dd-auth-001.question: What local state does auth need?
dd-auth-001.decision: AuthUser (cached user) and AuthSession (current session) entities. External Supabase owns data; local state enables reactive UI.
dd-auth-001.rationale: Following local state pattern - we cache what we need for reactive UI without duplicating Supabase's user database. Session entity tracks tokens for refresh awareness.

dd-auth-002.question: Where do isLoading and error states live?
dd-auth-002.decision: NOT in schema. Use volatile MST state in enhanceRootStore for cross-component coordination.
dd-auth-002.rationale: Domain purity principle - loading/error are UI concerns. Volatile state avoids persistence while enabling reactive observation.

dd-auth-003.question: What enhancement hooks will the domain need?
dd-auth-003.decision: enhanceModels: AuthSession.isExpired (computed from expiresAt); enhanceCollections: none needed; enhanceRootStore: currentUser (view), currentSession (view), isAuthenticated (view), authStatus (volatile: idle|loading|error), authError (volatile), signUp/signIn/signOut (actions that call IAuthService and sync state), initialize (action to restore session on app start)
dd-auth-003.rationale: All hooks implemented in single domain.ts using createStoreFromScope(). Views derive from cached entities. Actions coordinate with IAuthService from environment and sync results to local state. Volatile state tracks operation status without persistence.

dd-auth-004.question: How does auth service integrate with MST environment?
dd-auth-004.decision: Add optional services.auth: IAuthService to IEnvironment. Auth actions in enhanceRootStore access via getEnv(self).services.auth.
dd-auth-004.rationale: Follows existing environment pattern from IPersistenceService. Optional because not all stores need auth. Enables swapping Supabase/Mock implementations.
